  "findings": [
    "Condensate line is installed without a trap",
    "Condensate line is sloped incorrectly, preventing proper drainage"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install a proper condensate trap according to manufacturer specifications",
    "Re-route and secure the condensate line to ensure a continuous downward slope of at least 1/4 inch per foot toward the drain"
  ],
  "rationale": "A high-efficiency condensing furnace requires a properly trapped and sloped condensate drain line. Operating without a trap can allow flue gases to escape into the living space or cause pressure switch faults. Incorrect slope prevents proper drainage, leading to water backup, furnace shutdown, or property damage.",
  "references": [
    "IRC M1801",
    "NFPA 54",
    "Manufacturer Installation Instructions"
  ]
}
